title: palhinhaea cernua
intro: Palhinhaea cernua is a species of liverwort, a non-vascular plant belonging to the class Marchantiophyta. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Palhinhaea cernua include relieving congestion, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In traditional herbal preparations, Palhinhaea cernua is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and poultices that can be applied topically or taken orally to reap its benefits.
